notes.content: The B.A. program consists of a comprehensive study of acting, directing, design, music theatre, playwriting, stage management, theatre production, and dramatic theory, criticism, literature, and history. It involves hands-on participation in productions as an actor, designer, writer, technician, or stage manager. Auditions for all productions at UCI are open to undergraduates, and some productions are open exclusively to undergraduates. The B.A. program does not require an entrance audition. There can be few subjects as broad or as fundamental to human civilization as those of Drama and Theatre. The B.A. in Drama is part of a larger education which includes close consideration of world history and politics; human psychology and desire; music, dance, literature and art; visual and aural aesthetics, and both ancient and contemporary technologies of communication. While Drama is rightfully considered an entertainment, it is an entertainment that begins with the greatest literature ever written – from the classics of Euripides, Shakespeare and Molière to modern Americans like Tennessee Williams, August Wilson and David Henry Hwang – as presented in the most sophisticated performance techniques from Europe, Asia, Africa, and the Americas developed over 3,000 years. And Theatre – the practice of Drama – is a hands-on collaboration that involves real-time interactions with other artists, and displaying the results to real-life audiences. This major does not require completion of prerequisites for admission as a transfer student. NOTE : If not completed prior to transfer, Drama 10, and 5 courses chosen from Drama 30B, 30C, 50A, 50B, 50C, 50D, 50E must be completed the first year in residence at UCI. One of the 5 courses must be from Drama 50C, 50D, or 50E. It is recommended that Drama 40A, 40B, 40C be completed as soon as possible after transfer to UCI. Special Advising Note: Transfers students are strongly encouraged to complete as many preparatory courses as possible for their major before enrolling at UCI to streamline their path to graduation. NOTE : In fulfillment of the requirements below, a single course may be used only once. For information regarding the AP and IB examination credit policies refer to the UCI General Catalogue. For more information regarding the UC Irvine Transfer Admission Guarantee Program, please visit TAG
notes.contentHtml:
The B.A. program consists of a comprehensive study of acting, directing, design, music theatre, playwriting, stage management, theatre production, and dramatic theory, criticism, literature, and history. It involves hands-on participation in productions as an actor, designer, writer, technician, or stage manager. Auditions for all productions at UCI are open to undergraduates, and some productions are open exclusively to undergraduates. The B.A. program does not require an entrance audition.


There can be few subjects as broad or as fundamental to human civilization as those of Drama and Theatre. The B.A. in Drama is part of a larger education which includes close consideration of world history and politics; human psychology and desire; music, dance, literature and art; visual and aural aesthetics, and both ancient and contemporary technologies of communication. While Drama is rightfully considered an entertainment, it is an entertainment that begins with the greatest literature ever written – from the classics of Euripides, Shakespeare and Molière to modern Americans like Tennessee Williams, August Wilson and David Henry Hwang – as presented in the most sophisticated performance techniques from Europe, Asia, Africa, and the Americas developed over 3,000 years. And Theatre – the practice of Drama – is a hands-on collaboration that involves real-time interactions with other artists, and displaying the results to real-life audiences.


This major does not require completion of prerequisites for admission as a transfer student.


NOTE: If not completed prior to transfer, Drama 10, and 5 courses chosen from Drama 30B, 30C, 50A, 50B, 50C, 50D, 50E must be completed the first year in residence at UCI. One of the 5 courses must be from Drama 50C, 50D, or 50E. It is recommended that Drama 40A, 40B, 40C be completed as soon as possible after transfer to UCI.


Special Advising Note: Transfers students are strongly encouraged to complete as many preparatory courses as possible for their major before enrolling at UCI to streamline their path to graduation.


NOTE: In fulfillment of the requirements below, a single course may be used only once.


For information regarding the AP and IB examination credit policies refer to the UCI General Catalogue. 


For more information regarding the UC Irvine Transfer Admission Guarantee Program, please visit TAG
